description.content:
Returns OK when the server and MongoDB are reachable.
Load balancers should treat **503** as unhealthy.

response.name: Server and database are healthy
Database unavailable
User created
response.body:
{
  "data": {
    "_id": "664a1b2c3d4e5f678901234",
    "firstName": "Jane",
    "lastName": "Doe",
    "username": "jane",
    "email": "jane@example.com",
    "bio": "string",
    "status": "active",
    "createdAt": "1949-12-22T19:32:40.192Z",
    "updatedAt": "1949-06-10T10:50:17.975Z"
  },
  "message": "User registered successfully"
}

response.name: Validation failed
Email or username already in use
JSON body exceeds JSON_BODY_LIMIT
Too many registration attempts from this IP

item.name: Log in with email or username

request.name: Log in with email or username

description.content:
Send a single `identifier` (email or username) and `password`.
Returns a JWT in `token` — use it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on protected routes.

response.name: Login successful
Validation failed or invalid credentials
JSON body exceeds JSON_BODY_LIMIT
Too many login attempts from this IP

description.content:
Send the user's email and the full frontend reset route (`resetUrl`).
The server appends `?token=...` (or `&token=...`) and emails the link.
Always returns the same success message to avoid email enumeration.

response.name: Generic success (sent if email is registered)
Validation failed
JSON body exceeds JSON_BODY_LIMIT
Too many password reset requests from this IP

item.name: Set a new password with a reset token

request.name: Set a new password with a reset token

description.content:
Use the `token` query param from the reset link emailed to the user.
On success, log in separately with the new password.

response.name: Password updated
Validation failed or invalid/expired token
JSON body exceeds JSON_BODY_LIMIT
Too many reset attempts from this IP

description.content:
Multipart upload using field name `files` (repeat for multiple files).
Storage backend is selected via `UPLOAD_DRIVER` (`local`, `s3`, `cloudinary`).

formdata.description: (Required) One or more files (field name must be `files`)

response.name: Files uploaded

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
(Required) One or more files (field name must be `files`)

  "data": [

      "id": "664a1b2c3d4e5f678901234567",

response.name: Upload rate limit exceeded

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
(Required) One or more files (field name must be `files`)

Moves a file out of the active storage location so clients can no longer access it
at the original URL. The file is retained under an archive prefix/folder for
server-side recovery (`UPLOAD_ARCHIVE_PREFIX`, default `_archive`).
Only the user who uploaded the file may archive it.
Pass the MongoDB `id` from the upload response (recommended), or the stored `name`.
For Cloudinary `public_id` values that contain `/`, prefer `id` or URL-encode the name.

variable.description: (Required) File `id` from the upload response, or stored `name`
